reason: not materially faster than task-clock on bare metal and catastrophically slower under virtualization
selection_rule: a candidate must beat the incumbent by more than max(1 ns/read, 5%) in at least 8 of 9 paired batches

historical_probe_caveat.problem: the old probe excluded kernel time and omitted arm64 config1=3, so it was not an eligible comparison
historical_probe_caveat.correction: the recorded probe requests user access and 64-bit PMCCNTR with config1=3, includes user and kernel execution, excludes hypervisor time, checks the complete mmap capability state, uses the perf seqlock+offset protocol, converts at the fixed Graviton frequency, and checks busy/sleep parity

not_covered: forced migration; the reproducible probe now includes it, but the recorded VM and metal runs predate that addition

conclusion: Do not add PMCCNTR_EL0 as an eligible ThreadCpuInstant production candidate. Capability is not profitability: cap_user_rdpmc was true in both cells, yet virtualization made the route about 23x slower and bare metal produced only a sub-nanosecond material tie.
